mechanism.","errors":{"ERC20InsufficientAllowance(address,uint256,uint256)":[{"details":"Indicates a failure with the `spender`\u2019s `allowance`. NOTE: If `value` is the maximum `uint256`, the allowance is not updated on `transferFrom`. This is semantically equivalent to an infinite approval. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address."},"balanceOf(address)":{"details":"See {IERC20-balanceOf}."},"claimAllRewards()":{"details":"More gas efficient than calling both claim functions separately"},"claimEthRewards()":{"details":"Workers and validators can call this function to claim their unclaimed ETH rewards"},"claimTokenRewards()":{"details":"Workers and validators can call this function to claim their unclaimed SNO token rewards"},"decimals()":{"details":"Returns the number of decimals used to get its user representation. For example, if `decimals` equals `2`, a balance of `505` tokens should be displayed to a user as `5.05` (`505 / 10 ** 2`). Tokens usually opt for a value of 18, imitating the relationship between Ether and Wei. This is the default value returned by this function, unless it's overridden. NOTE: This information is only used for _display_ purposes: it in no way affects any of the arithmetic of the contract, including {IERC20-balanceOf} and NOTE: Does not update the allowance if the current allowance is the maximum `uint256`. Requirements: - `from` and `to` cannot be the zero address. - `from` must have a balance of at least `value`. - the caller must have allowance for ``from``'s tokens of at least `value`."},"transferOwnership(address)":{"details":"Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). approve(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-approve}. NOTE: If `value` is the maximum `uint256`, the allowance is not updated on `transferFrom`. This is semantically equivalent to an infinite approval. Requirements: - `spender` cannot be the zero address.

claimAllRewards().details: More gas efficient than calling both claim functions separately

claimEthRewards().details: Workers and validators can call this function to claim their unclaimed ETH rewards

claimTokenRewards().details: Workers and validators can call this function to claim their unclaimed SNO token rewards

decimals().details: Returns the number of decimals used to get its user representation. For example, if `decimals` equals `2`, a balance of `505` tokens should be displayed to a user as `5.05` (`505 / 10 ** 2`). Tokens usually opt for a value of 18, imitating the relationship between Ether and Wei. This is the default value returned by this function, unless it's overridden. NOTE: This information is only used for _display_ purposes: it in no way affects any of the arithmetic of the contract, including {IERC20-balanceOf} and {IERC20-transfer}.

params._networkId: The ID of the network for which the payment is being made
params._payment: The amount of ETH payment to be escrowed
params._user: The address of the user making the payment

params._networkId: The ID of the network for which the payment is being made
params._payment: The amount of payment to be escrowed
params._user: The address of the user receiving the payment

returns.ethBalance: Total ETH held by the contract

getEscrowedPayments(address).details: Get escrowed payments for a specific address

getLockAmounts().details: Get the lock amounts for validators and users

getLockedTokens(address).details: Get locked token information for a specific address

getSmartnodesCore().details: Get the current SmartnodesCore contract address

returns.totalUnclaimed: Total unclaimed SNO tokens (will be minted when claimed)

getTotalClaimed(address).details: Get total claimed rewards for a specific address

getUnclaimedRewards(address).details: Get unclaimed rewards for a specific address

isUserLocked(address).details: Check if a user has tokens locked

params._user: The address of the validator locking tokens

mintRewards(address[],address[],uint256[.details: Workers receive 90% of the total reward, validators receive 10%Rewards are distributed evenly among validators who voted, and distributed proportionally to workers based on their capacitiesWorkers and validators can claim their rewards laterThis function is called by SmartnodesCore during state updates to distribute rewards

params._capacities: Array of capacities for each worker (not used in this implementation)
params._payments: Additional payments to be added to the current emission rate
params._validators: Array of validator addresses who voted
params._workers: Array of worker addresses who performed work

name().details: Returns the name of the token.

owner().details: Returns the address of the current owner.

releaseEscrowedEthPayment(address,uint25.details: This releases the escrowed ETH to be available for distribution as rewardsThe ETH stays in the contract but is no longer considered "escrowed"

params._amount: The amount of escrowed ETH payment to be released
params._user: The address of the user whose escrowed ETH payment is being released

releaseEscrowedPayment(address,uint256).details: Can only be called by SmartnodesCore

params._amount: The amount of payment to be released
params._user: The address of the user who made the payment

renounceOwnership().details: Leaves the contract without owner. It will not be possible to call `onlyOwner` functions. Can only be called by the current owner. NOTE: Renouncing ownership will leave the contract without an owner, thereby disabling any functionality that is only available to the owner.

setSmartnodesCore(address).details: Sets the SmartnodesCore contract address. Can only be called once by owner.

params._smartnodesCore: Address of the deployed SmartnodesCore contract

symbol().details: Returns the symbol of the token, usually a shorter version of the name.

transfer(address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transfer}. Requirements: - `to` cannot be the zero address. - the caller must have a balance of at least `value`.

transferFrom(address,address,uint256).details: See {IERC20-transferFrom}. Skips emitting an {Approval} event indicating an allowance update. This is not required by the ERC. See {xref-ERC20-_approve-address-address-uint256-bool-}[_approve]. NOTE: Does not update the allowance if the current allowance is the maximum `uint256`. Requirements: - `from` and `to` cannot be the zero address. - `from` must have a balance of at least `value`. - the caller must have allowance for ``from``'s tokens of at least `value`.

transferOwnership(address).details: Transfers ownership of the contract to a new account (`newOwner`). Can only be called by the current owner.

unlockTokens(address).details: Can be called once to initiate the unlock process, and once again for claiming tokens after the unlock period

params._user: The address of the user unlocking tokens

claimAllRewards().notice: Claim both SNO token and ETH rewards in a single transaction

claimEthRewards().notice: Claim unclaimed ETH rewards for a worker or validator

claimTokenRewards().notice: Claim unclaimed SNO token rewards for a worker or validator

escrowEthPayment(address,uint256,uint8).notice: Escrow ETH payment for a job

getDaoFunds().notice: Get DAO funds (both SNO and ETH)

getEmissionRate().notice: Get current emission rate based on era

getEthBreakdown().notice: Get comprehensive breakdown of ETH in the contract

getSupply().notice: Get simple supply metrics (legacy function - kept for compatibility)

getSupplyBreakdown().notice: Get comprehensive breakdown of SNO token supply

getTotalUnclaimed().notice: Get total unclaimed rewards (both SNO and ETH)

lockTokens(address,bool).notice: Lock tokens for a validator or user

mintRewards(address[],address[],uint256[.notice: Distribute rewards to workers and validators

releaseEscrowedEthPayment(address,uint25.notice: Release escrowed ETH payment for reward distribution

releaseEscrowedPayment(address,uint256).notice: Release escrowed payment to distribute as rewards

unlockTokens(address).notice: Unlock tokens for a user after the unlock period
